summary: Dalam pemilihan pendahuluan Partai Demokrat di Maine, Graham Platter yang dilanda skandal mengalahkan Gubernur Janet Mills dengan sekitar 75% suara, dan akan menantang Senator Partai Republik Susan Collins. Platter perlu memenangkan pendukung Mills, sementara Collins masih unggul di kalangan pemilih independen. Berita lainnya: AS melancarkan serangan rudal ke Iran; OpenAI mengajukan IPO; sidang dokumen Epstein; pernyataan kontroversial kandidat gubernur Colorado Victor Marx.

vocabulary.en: to continue to support or choose someone or something
vocabulary.excerpt: Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner.
vocabulary.example_cn: Para pemilih Demokrat di Maine memutuskan untuk terus mendukung Graham Platter.

vocabulary.cn: membalikkan (merujuk pada partai yang memenangkan kursi yang sebelumnya dikuasai lawan dalam pemilu)
vocabulary.en: to win a seat or office that was previously held by the opposing party
vocabulary.excerpt: Could that cost the party a shot at flipping the Senate this fall?
vocabulary.example_cn: Akankah ini melemahkan peluang partai untuk membalikkan Senat pada musim gugur ini?

vocabulary.en: to consider something carefully, especially by comparing different factors
vocabulary.excerpt: that's the situation that we have to weigh.
vocabulary.example_cn: Itu adalah situasi yang harus kita pertimbangkan.

vocabulary.en: to cause something to be under pressure or tension
vocabulary.excerpt: Will the strained ceasefire last long enough for the U.S. to deliver that long-promised deal with Iran?
vocabulary.example_cn: Akankah gencatan senjata yang tegang bertahan cukup lama bagi AS untuk menepati perjanjian komitmen jangka panjang dengan Iran?

vocabulary.en: to fulfill a promise or expectation; to provide something promised
vocabulary.excerpt: for the U.S. to deliver that long-promised deal with Iran.
vocabulary.example_cn: agar AS menepati perjanjian komitmen jangka panjang dengan Iran.

vocabulary.en: the person or thing that is most likely to win a competition or election
vocabulary.excerpt: Meet the frontrunner in the Republican race for governor of Colorado.
vocabulary.example_cn: Kenali pemimpin dalam pemilihan gubernur Partai Republik di Colorado.

vocabulary.cn: gelembung (kenaikan harga aset yang cepat terlepas dari fundamental)
vocabulary.en: a situation in which the price of an asset rises far above its real value, often followed by a sudden collapse
vocabulary.excerpt: signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.example_cn: Menunjukkan bahwa gelembung AI akan segera pecah.

vocabulary.en: to burst or break open suddenly, often with a sound
vocabulary.excerpt: the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.example_cn: 人工智能泡沫即将破裂。

vocabulary.cn: 获得（提名/资格）；成功晋级
vocabulary.en: to secure a nomination or qualification, especially in politics
vocabulary.excerpt: Graham Platner punches his ticket.
vocabulary.example_cn: 格雷厄姆·普拉特纳获得了提名。

vocabulary.en: to finally win or achieve something, especially after a struggle
vocabulary.excerpt: Moments after clinching the nomination, he turned his attention to his new opponent.

vocabulary.en: lacking courage or determination; weak-willed
vocabulary.excerpt: she has become just as spineless and corrupt as the establishment she now serves.
vocabulary.example_cn: 她变得和她现在服务的建制派一样懦弱和腐败。

vocabulary.en: the group of people who have power and influence in a society or organization, often seen as conservative or resistant to change
vocabulary.excerpt: as the establishment she now serves.
vocabulary.example_cn: 她所服务的建制派。

vocabulary.cn: 独立的声音（指不依附政党、独立表达意见的人）
vocabulary.en: a person or entity that expresses opinions independently, not aligned with a particular party or group
vocabulary.excerpt: If you are an independent voice, why do you vote with Donald Trump 95 percent of the time?

vocabulary.word: have a dog in the fight
vocabulary.cn: 在竞争中有利益关系；参与其中
vocabulary.en: to have a personal stake or interest in a particular situation or conflict
vocabulary.excerpt: you guys had a dog in the fight in this race.
vocabulary.example_cn: 你们在这场竞选中有利害关系。

vocabulary.en: at risk; something that could be lost or gained
vocabulary.excerpt: what's at stake here.
vocabulary.example_cn: Apa yang dipertaruhkan di sini.

vocabulary.en: to put something at risk of being lost or harmed
vocabulary.excerpt: Maybe this has jeopardized it.
vocabulary.example_cn: Mungkin ini sudah membahayakannya.

vocabulary.en: an easy or decisive victory, especially in an election or sports event
vocabulary.excerpt: A lot of times it can be a blowout, right?
vocabulary.example_cn: Seringkali itu bisa menjadi kemenangan telak, bukan?

vocabulary.en: fundamentally; in its most important aspects
vocabulary.excerpt: we're saying the same thing in essence, right?
vocabulary.example_cn: 我们本质上说的是同一件事，对吧？

vocabulary.en: difficult to defeat or overcome
vocabulary.excerpt: Susan Collins is tough to beat.
vocabulary.example_cn: 苏珊·柯林斯很难被击败。

vocabulary.cn: Susan Collins versi yang lebih ringan (kandidat dengan posisi serupa tetapi lebih moderat)

vocabulary.excerpt: you're not going to win this seat by electing a Susan Collins light.
vocabulary.example_cn: Kamu tidak bisa memenangkan kursi ini dengan memilih Susan Collins versi yang lebih ringan.

vocabulary.en: to motivate and rally the core supporters of a political party or candidate
vocabulary.excerpt: You are going to beat her by energizing your base.
vocabulary.example_cn: Kamu harus mengalahkannya dengan memotivasi basis pemilihmu.

vocabulary.cn: Pemilihan paruh waktu (pemilihan kongres yang diadakan di tengah masa jabatan presiden AS)
vocabulary.en: an election held in the middle of a president's term, typically for Congress and state offices
vocabulary.excerpt: particularly in a midterm election.
vocabulary.example_cn: Terutama dalam pemilihan paruh waktu.

vocabulary.en: a direct vote by the electorate on a specific proposal or issue
vocabulary.excerpt: this election has got to be a referendum on Susan Collins.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这次选举必须是对苏珊·柯林斯的公投。

vocabulary.en: to require someone to explain their actions and accept responsibility
vocabulary.excerpt: her refusal to hold Donald Trump accountable.
vocabulary.example_cn: 她拒绝追究唐纳德·特朗普的责任。

vocabulary.en: consecutively; one after another
vocabulary.excerpt: I want to play two clips back-to-back.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我想连续播放两段剪辑。

vocabulary.cn: 强有力的支持；明确的背书
vocabulary.en: a strong and enthusiastic statement of support
vocabulary.excerpt: these are not ringing endorsements.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这些都不是强有力的支持。

vocabulary.en: without her consent or desire
vocabulary.excerpt: who said she voted for Susan Collins, against her will.
vocabulary.example_cn: 她说她违背自己的意愿投票给了苏珊·柯林斯。

vocabulary.en: the moral and ethical qualities of a person
vocabulary.excerpt: It's really about the character quality.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这实际上是关于品格素质。

vocabulary.en: a close and strong relationship
vocabulary.excerpt: Collins and Trump, that's not a tight bond.
vocabulary.example_cn: 柯林斯和特朗普，关系并不紧密。

vocabulary.en: a mechanism that initiates a process or event
vocabulary.excerpt: The 60 days is a trigger.
vocabulary.example_cn: 60天是一个触发点。

vocabulary.en: formal consent or authorization by the U.S. Congress
vocabulary.excerpt: the president has to obtain congressional approval.
vocabulary.example_cn: 总统必须获得国会批准。

vocabulary.en: found guilty of a crime by a court of law
vocabulary.excerpt: individuals who were convicted of violence against police officers.
vocabulary.example_cn: Seseorang yang telah dihukum karena menyerang polisi dengan kekerasan.

vocabulary.en: the act of paying back money spent or lost
vocabulary.excerpt: should be entitled to reimbursement for their legal fees.
vocabulary.example_cn: Harus berhak mengganti biaya hukum mereka.

vocabulary.en: charges for legal services provided by a lawyer or law firm
vocabulary.excerpt: reimbursement for their legal fees.
vocabulary.example_cn: Mengganti biaya hukum mereka.

vocabulary.en: reductions in spending, especially in government budgets
vocabulary.excerpt: Concerned about the cuts in Medicaid.
vocabulary.example_cn: Khawatir tentang pemotongan Medicaid.

vocabulary.cn: Medicaid (asuransi kesehatan untuk orang berpenghasilan rendah yang disediakan oleh pemerintah federal dan negara bagian AS)
vocabulary.en: a U.S. government program that provides health insurance to low-income individuals and families
vocabulary.excerpt: Concerned about the cuts in Medicaid.
vocabulary.example_cn: Khawatir tentang pemotongan Medicaid.

vocabulary.cn: 执法激增；加强执法行动
vocabulary.en: a sudden increase in law enforcement activities, often targeting specific issues
vocabulary.excerpt: a pause on the enforcement surge in both Maine and in Minne...
vocabulary.example_cn: 暂停缅因州和明尼苏达州的执法激增行动。

vocabulary.en: a weapon that is thrown or fired through the air, especially one that is guided
vocabulary.excerpt: Missiles light up the sky in the Middle East again.
vocabulary.example_cn: 导弹再次照亮中东的天空。

vocabulary.en: a temporary agreement to stop fighting
vocabulary.excerpt: Will the strained ceasefire last long enough?
vocabulary.example_cn: Apakah gencatan senjata yang tegang ini dapat bertahan cukup lama?

vocabulary.level: Latar belakang nama khusus · Tingkat lanjut
vocabulary.cn: Dokumen Epstein (merujuk pada dokumen kasus yang terkait dengan pelaku kejahatan seksual yang telah meninggal, Jeffrey Epstein)
vocabulary.en: documents related to the case of Jeffrey Epstein, a convicted sex offender
vocabulary.excerpt: a big day for the Epstein files.
vocabulary.example_cn: Hari penting bagi dokumen Epstein.

vocabulary.en: a person who has been convicted of a sexual crime

vocabulary.cn: gubernatorial; berkaitan dengan gubernur atau pemilihan gubernur
vocabulary.en: relating to a governor or the election of a governor
vocabulary.excerpt: Colorado gubernatorial candidate.

vocabulary.en: Initial Public Offering; the first sale of a company's shares to the public
vocabulary.excerpt: OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs.
vocabulary.example_cn: OpenAI dan Anthropic berencana melakukan Penawaran Umum Perdana.

vocabulary.en: a situation where an economic bubble is about to burst
vocabulary.excerpt: the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.example_cn: 人工智能泡沫即将破裂。

vocabulary.en: to indicate or show something, often indirectly
vocabulary.excerpt: signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.example_cn: 向我表明人工智能泡沫即将破裂。

vocabulary.en: a helicopter that has been shot down or forced to crash
vocabulary.excerpt: U.S. Hits Iran after Helicopter Downed.
vocabulary.example_cn: 直升机被击落后美国打击伊朗。

vocabulary.en: to attack or strike, especially militarily
vocabulary.excerpt: U.S. Hits Iran after Helicopter Downed.
vocabulary.example_cn: Setelah helikopter ditembak jatuh, Amerika menyerang Iran.

vocabulary.en: a person who makes laws; a member of a legislature
vocabulary.excerpt: What will Bill Gates tell lawmakers about his links?
vocabulary.example_cn: Apa yang akan Bill Gates katakan kepada para legislator tentang hubungannya dengan Epstein?

vocabulary.en: connections or relationships between people or things
vocabulary.excerpt: his links to the late convicted sex offender.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他与已故的已定罪性犯罪者的联系。

vocabulary.en: to have committed murder when one was a child
vocabulary.excerpt: a candidate who also claims he killed someone as a child.
vocabulary.example_cn: 一位还声称自己小时候杀过人的候选人。

sentences.en: Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner.
sentences.cn: 缅因州的民主党人投票支持格雷厄姆·普拉特纳。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（Democrats in Maine）+ 谓语（vote）+ 不定式短语（to stick with Graham Platner）作宾语。
sentences.analysis: stick with 意为"坚持支持"，是新闻常用短语。

sentences.en: Could that cost the party a shot at flipping the Senate this fall?
sentences.cn: 这会让该党失去今年秋天翻转参议院的机会吗？
sentences.structure: 疑问句：情态动词（Could）+ 主语（that）+ 谓语（cost）+ 间接宾语（the party）+ 直接宾语（a shot at flipping the Senate this fall）。
sentences.analysis: cost sb. a shot at sth. 意为"使某人失去做某事的机会"；flip the Senate 指"翻转参议院（控制权）"，是政治新闻常用表达。

sentences.en: If the choice on the ballot is between that and a senator who's voted to take health care away from millions of Americans, that's the situation that we have to weigh.
sentences.cn: 如果选票上的选择是在那个（指普拉特纳的争议行为）和一位投票剥夺数百万美国人医保的参议员之间，那就是我们必须权衡的情况。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（If...）+ 主句（that's the situation）+ 定语从句（that we have to weigh）。
sentences.analysis: weigh 意为"权衡"，是抽象动词；take health care away from 是新闻中常见的负面政策描述。

sentences.en: Will the strained ceasefire last long enough for the U.S. to deliver that long-promised deal with Iran?
sentences.cn: 紧张的停火协议能否持续足够长的时间，让美国兑现与伊朗长期承诺的协议？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句：Will + 主语（the strained ceasefire）+ 谓语（last）+ 状语（long enough）+ 不定式短语（for the U.S. to deliver...）。
sentences.analysis: strained ceasefire 意为"紧张的停火"；long-promised 是复合形容词，意为"长期承诺的"。

sentences.en: What will Bill Gates tell lawmakers about his links to the late convicted sex offender.
sentences.cn: 比尔·盖茨将就他与已故被定罪性犯罪者的关联向立法者说些什么。
sentences.structure: 特殊疑问句：What + 助动词（will）+ 主语（Bill Gates）+ 谓语（tell）+ 间接宾语（lawmakers）+ 介词短语（about his links...）。
sentences.analysis: the late convicted sex offender 指"已故被定罪的性犯罪者"，late 意为"已故的"，convicted 是过去分词作定语。

sentences.en: Do you think that you've killed people as an adult?
sentences.cn: 你认为你成年后杀过人吗？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句（Do you think）+ 宾语从句（that you've killed people as an adult）。
sentences.analysis: as an adult 意为"作为成年人"，与后文"as a child"形成对比。

sentences.en: OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs at release before the end of this year, signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
sentences.cn: OpenAI 和 Anthropic 计划在今年年底前进行首次公开募股，这向我表明人工智能泡沫即将破裂。
sentences.structure: 主句（OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs...）+ 现在分词短语（signaling to me that...）作结果状语，其中包含宾语从句（that the A.I. bubble is about to pop）。
sentences.analysis: IPO 是 Initial Public Offering 的缩写；signal to sb. that... 意为"向某人表明……"；be about to pop 意为"即将破裂"。

sentences.en: To any of those who feel let down or disappointed or disillusioned, it is my job to earn your trust, faith, and support.
sentences.cn: 对于任何感到失望、沮丧或幻想破灭的人，我的工作是赢得你们的信任、信念和支持。
sentences.structure: 介词短语（To any of those...）作状语，其中包含定语从句（who feel let down or disappointed or disillusioned）；主句（it is my job to earn...）。
sentences.analysis: let down 意为"失望"；disillusioned 意为"幻想破灭"；it is my job to do sth. 是强调结构的常见表达。

sentences.en: And I will spend every day of this campaign.
sentences.cn: 我将花费这场竞选的每一天。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（I）+ 谓语（will spend）+ 宾语（every day of this campaign）。
sentences.analysis: spend 后接时间名词，表示"花费时间"。

sentences.en: And if I have the privilege every day in the United States Senate doing exactly that.
sentences.cn: 如果我有幸每天在美国参议院做同样的事。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（if I have the privilege...），其中包含现在分词短语（doing exactly that）作伴随状语。
sentences.analysis: have the privilege doing sth. 意为"有幸做某事"；the United States Senate 是专有名词。

sentences.en: Graham Platner punches his ticket.
sentences.cn: 格雷厄姆·普拉特纳获得了提名。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（Graham Platner）+ 谓语（punches）+ 宾语（his ticket）。
sentences.analysis: punch one's ticket 是习语，意为"获得（政治）提名或资格"。

sentences.en: But now he's got to convince voters to choose him over a five-term senator.
sentences.cn: 但现在他必须说服选民选择他而不是一位连任五届的参议员。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（he）+ 谓语（'s got to convince）+ 宾语（voters）+ 不定式短语（to choose him over...）。
sentences.analysis: convince sb. to do sth. 意为"说服某人做某事"；choose A over B 意为"选择A而不是B"；five-term 是复合形容词，意为"连任五届的"。

sentences.en: Democrat Graham Platner, despite all the scandals, earned about three-fourths of primary voters' support over Governor Janet Mills.
sentences.cn: 民主党人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳，尽管有所有丑闻，还是赢得了大约四分之三初选选民的支持，超过了州长珍妮特·米尔斯。
sentences.structure: 主语（Democrat Graham Platner）+ 插入语（despite all the scandals）+ 谓语（earned）+ 宾语（about three-fourths of primary voters' support）+ 状语（over Governor Janet Mills）。
sentences.analysis: despite 表示让步；three-fourths 是分数表达；over 在此表示"超过"。

sentences.en: Moments after clinching the nomination, he turned his attention to his new opponent, Republican Senator Susan Collins.
sentences.cn: 在获得提名后不久，他就将注意力转向了他的新对手，共和党参议员苏珊·柯林斯。
sentences.structure: 时间状语（获得提名后不久）+ 主句（他将注意力转向...），其中宾语（他的新对手）后跟同位语（共和党参议员苏珊·柯林斯）。
sentences.analysis: clinch the nomination 意为"获得提名"；turn one's attention to 意为"将注意力转向"。

sentences.en: Susan Collins may have started her career decades ago in Washington with good intentions, but she has become just as spineless and corrupt as the establishment she now serves.
sentences.cn: 苏珊·柯林斯可能几十年前在华盛顿以良好的意图开始了她的职业生涯，但她已经变得和她现在所服务的建制派一样软弱和腐败。
sentences.structure: 并列句：第一分句（Susan Collins may have started...）+ but 连接第二分句（she has become...），其中包含比较结构（as...as...）和定语从句（she now serves）。
sentences.analysis: may have started 表示对过去的推测；spineless 意为"没有骨气的"；the establishment 指"建制派"。

sentences.en: If you are an independent voice, why do you vote with Donald Trump 95 percent of the time?
sentences.cn: 如果你是一个独立的声音，为什么你95%的时间都和唐纳德·特朗普投票一致？
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（If you are...）+ 主句（why do you vote...）。
sentences.analysis: independent voice 意为"独立的声音"；vote with sb. 意为"与某人投相同票"。

sentences.en: Platner has a lot of work to do to get Mills voters on his side.
sentences.cn: 普拉特纳有很多工作要做，以争取米尔斯选民的支持。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（Platner）+ 谓语（has）+ 宾语（a lot of work）+ 不定式短语（to do to get...）作定语和目的状语。
sentences.analysis: get sb. on one's side 意为"争取某人支持"。

sentences.en: I couldn't honestly support Platner, even though some of the things that he says are really good.
sentences.cn: 我无法诚实地支持普拉特纳，尽管他说的一些话确实很好。
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama (I couldn't honestly support Platner) + klausa konsesif (even though some of the things...), yang mengandung klausa relatif (that he says).
sentences.analysis: honestly adalah adverbia, memodifikasi support; even though memimpin klausa konsesif.

sentences.en: I don't think he's ready to be a senator.
sentences.cn: Saya rasa dia belum siap menjadi senator.
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama (I don't think) + klausa objek (he's ready to be a senator).
sentences.analysis: Negasi transfer: kalimat utama negatif, klausa positif.

sentences.en: I'm leaning towards Collins unless the guy shows something else, but I just -- he's not there yet.
sentences.cn: Saya cenderung ke Collins, kecuali orang itu menunjukkan hal lain, tapi saya hanya -- dia belum sampai di situ.
sentences.structure: Kalimat majemuk: klausa pertama (I'm leaning towards Collins unless...) + but menghubungkan klausa kedua (I just -- he's not there yet).
sentences.analysis: lean towards berarti 'cenderung'; unless memimpin klausa syarat; he's not there yet adalah ekspresi informal, berarti 'dia belum mencapai standar'.

sentences.en: And so, what will you do in November if its Platner versus Collins?
sentences.cn: Jadi, jika November adalah Platner versus Collins, apa yang akan Anda lakukan?
sentences.structure: Kalimat tanya khusus (what will you do...) + klausa syarat (if it's Platner versus Collins).
sentences.analysis: its seharusnya it's (kesalahan ketik); versus berarti 'melawan'.

sentences.en: I'm a Democrat, but I voted for Susan Collins several times, because I think that she does care about the state of Maine.
sentences.cn: Saya seorang Demokrat, tetapi saya telah memilih Susan Collins berkali-kali karena saya pikir dia benar-benar peduli pada Maine.
sentences.structure: 并列句：第一分句（I'm a Democrat）+ but 连接第二分句（I voted for Susan Collins several times），后跟原因状语从句（because I think...），其中包含宾语从句（that she does care...）。
sentences.analysis: does 表示强调；care about 意为"关心"。

sentences.en: But it's going to be a real problem for me in November.
sentences.cn: 但这在十一月对我来说将是一个真正的问题。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（it）+ 谓语（'s going to be）+ 表语（a real problem）+ 状语（for me in November）。
sentences.analysis: be going to 表示将来；a real problem 意为"一个真正的问题"。

sentences.en: And Max, I'm actually going to start with you, because you're with VoteVets, which means you guys had a dog in the fight in this race.
sentences.cn: 马克斯，我实际上要从你开始，因为你在 VoteVets，这意味着你们在这场竞选中有利害关系。
sentences.structure: 主句（I'm actually going to start with you）+ 原因状语从句（because you're with VoteVets），后跟非限制性定语从句（which means...），其中包含宾语从句（you guys had a dog in the fight）。
sentences.analysis: have a dog in the fight 是习语，意为"有利害关系"。

sentences.en: Democrats' chances of flipping the Maine Senate seat.
sentences.cn: 民主党翻转缅因州参议院席位的机会。
sentences.structure: 名词短语：主语（Democrats' chances）+ 介词短语（of flipping the Maine Senate seat）作定语。
sentences.analysis: flip the seat 意为"翻转席位（改变政党控制）"。

sentences.en: Is this race as close as you all hoped it would be, now that Graham has struggled with these scandals?
sentences.cn: 既然格雷厄姆因这些丑闻而挣扎，这场竞选是否像你们所有人希望的那样接近？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句（Is this race as close as...）+ 比较结构（as...as...）+ 宾语从句（you all hoped it would be）+ 原因状语从句（now that...）。
sentences.analysis: as close as 表示"和……一样接近"；now that 意为"既然"。

sentences.en: The race is always going to be close.
sentences.cn: 这场竞选总是会很接近。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（The race）+ 谓语（is always going to be）+ 表语（close）。
sentences.analysis: be going to 表示将来；close 在此意为"势均力敌的"。

sentences.en: Not with a five-term senator, it's not.
sentences.cn: 不是面对一位连任五届的参议员，它不是。
sentences.structure: 省略句：完整形式为"It is not close with a five-term senator."。
sentences.analysis: five-term 是复合形容词；it's not 是省略表达。

sentences.en: What I would say about the Susan Collins race is that we've seen in the past, is you're not going to win this seat by electing a Susan Collins light or putting forward a Susan Collins light in a race.
sentences.cn: 关于苏珊·柯林斯的竞选，我想说的是，我们在过去看到的是，你不会通过选举一个"苏珊·柯林斯轻量版"或在竞选中推出一个"苏珊·柯林斯轻量版"来赢得这个席位。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（What I would say...）+ 系动词（is）+ 表语从句（that we've seen...），其中表语从句又包含另一个表语从句（is you're not going to win...）。
sentences.analysis: Susan Collins light 意为"苏珊·柯林斯的弱化版"，light 表示"轻量级的"；put forward 意为"提出"。

sentences.en: You are going to beat her by energizing your base, particularly in a midterm election.
sentences.cn: 你将通过激励你的基本盘来击败她，尤其是在中期选举中。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（You）+ 谓语（are going to beat）+ 宾语（her）+ 方式状语（by energizing your base）+ 插入语（particularly in a midterm election）。
sentences.analysis: energize your base 意为"激励你的基本盘"；midterm election 指"中期选举"。

topics.title: 缅因州参议员选举：普拉特纳 vs. 柯林斯
topics.content: 缅因州民主党初选中，丑闻缠身的格雷厄姆·普拉特纳（Graham Platner）以约75%得票率击败州长珍妮特·米尔斯（Janet Mills），将挑战五届共和党参议员苏珊·柯林斯（Susan Collins）。普拉特纳需争取米尔斯支持者，而柯林斯在独立选民中仍有优势。民主党希望翻转该席位，但普拉特纳的品格问题可能影响选情。
topics.keywords: Graham Platner · Susan Collins · primary · flip · independent voter

topics.title: 美国对伊朗军事打击
topics.content: 美国在直升机被击落后对伊朗发动导弹袭击，中东紧张局势升级。此前美伊之间曾有长期承诺的协议，但停火（ceasefire）面临压力。事件可能影响地区稳定及美国外交政策。
topics.keywords: missile · Iran · ceasefire · U.S. military strike · helicopter downed

topics.title: 人工智能公司IPO与泡沫担忧
topics.content: OpenAI和Anthropic计划在年底前进行首次公开募股（IPO），引发市场对人工智能泡沫（A.I. bubble）即将破裂的担忧。投资者关注AI行业估值是否过高，以及IPO是否标志行业转折点。
topics.keywords: IPO · OpenAI · Anthropic · A.I. bubble · pop

topics.title: 科罗拉多州长候选人争议言论
topics.content: Calon gubernur Colorado dari Partai Republik, Victor Marx, mengaku pernah membunuh seseorang saat kecil dan bertanya "Apakah itu penting?", memicu kontroversi. Ia adalah frontrunner dalam pemilihan pendahuluan Partai Republik, dan pernyataan ekstremnya dapat memengaruhi hasil pemilu.
topics.keywords: Victor Marx · gubernatorial · frontrunner · controversial · killed someone

quiz.question: Dalam 'Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner', 'stick with' paling mendekati arti?
quiz.explanation: 'stick with' adalah phrasal verb yang berarti "terus mendukung atau memilih", di sini berarti para Demokrat memutuskan untuk terus mendukung Platner. Pilihan lain adalah arti harfiah atau kebalikannya.

quiz.question: Dalam 'the A.I. bubble is about to pop', arti 'pop' adalah?
quiz.explanation: 'pop' di sini berarti "pecah", merujuk pada pecahnya gelembung. Dalam konteks ekonomi, 'bubble pops' sering digunakan untuk menunjukkan pecahnya gelembung.

quiz.question: Dalam 'Graham Platner punches his ticket', apa arti 'punches his ticket'?
quiz.explanation: 'punch one's ticket' adalah idiom yang berarti "mendapatkan nominasi atau kualifikasi", terutama dalam konteks politik yang berarti berhasil maju ke tahap berikutnya.

quiz.question: Berdasarkan teks, setelah Platner memenangkan pemilihan pendahuluan, tantangan utama yang dihadapinya adalah?
quiz.explanation: Teks menyebutkan bahwa Platner perlu "get Mills voters on his side", yaitu mendapatkan dukungan dari pemilih Mills, karena Mills kalah dalam pemilihan pendahuluan dan pemilihnya mungkin beralih ke Collins.

quiz.question: Manakah pernyataan berikut yang benar tentang Susan Collins?
options: A. Dia memiliki hubungan erat dengan Trump
B. Dia adalah senator lima periode
D. Dia berasal dari Partai Demokrat
quiz.explanation: Teks menyebut Collins sebagai "five-term senator", yaitu senator lima periode. Hubungannya dengan Trump tidak erat ('not a tight bond'), dan dia adalah seorang Republikan.

quiz.question: Tentang IPO AI, pendapat apa yang dikutip dalam teks?
options: A. Gelembung AI akan segera pecah
C. IPO akan mendorong perkembangan AI
D. Pasar penuh keyakinan terhadap AI
quiz.explanation: Pendapat yang dikutip dalam teks adalah "the A.I. bubble is about to pop", yaitu gelembung AI akan segera pecah, mengisyaratkan bahwa IPO mungkin merupakan sinyal puncak gelembung.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Today in the group chat, Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner. Could that cost the party a shot at flipping the Senate this fall? REP. ALEXANDRIA OCASIO-CORTEZ (D-NY): If the choice on the ballot is between that and a senator who's voted to take health care away from millions of Americans, that's the situation that we have to weigh.
paragraphs.cn: Hari ini dalam obrolan grup, para Demokrat di Maine memilih Graham Platter. Akankah ini membuat mereka kehilangan kesempatan untuk membalikkan Senat musim gugur ini? Anggota DPR Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (Demokrat, New York): Jika pilihan di surat suara adalah antara dia dan seorang senator yang memilih untuk mencabut asuransi kesehatan jutaan warga Amerika, itulah situasi yang harus kita pertimbangkan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Missiles light up the sky in the Middle East again. Will the strained ceasefire last long enough for the U.S. to deliver that long- promised deal with Iran? And a big day for the Epstein files. What will Bill Gates tell lawmakers about his links to the late convicted sex offender.
paragraphs.cn: Rudal kembali menerangi langit Timur Tengah. Akankah gencatan senjata yang rapuh bertahan cukup lama bagi AS untuk menepati janji perjanjian jangka panjang dengan Iran? Dokumen Epstein memasuki hari penting. Apa yang akan dikatakan Bill Gates kepada para legislator tentang hubungannya dengan pelaku kejahatan seksual yang telah dihukum dan meninggal?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Do you think that you've killed people as an adult? VICTOR MARX (R),
paragraphs.cn: Apakah Anda pikir Anda pernah membunuh seseorang setelah dewasa? Victor Marx (Partai Republik),

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: This is a candidate who also claims he killed someone as a child. Meet the frontrunner in the Republican race for governor of Colorado. And it's the $3.5 trillion question on Wall Street. Has the A.I. boom gotten ahead of itself?
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah kandidat yang mengaku pernah membunuh seseorang saat kecil. Kenali calon terdepan dalam pemilihan pendahuluan gubernur Colorado dari Partai Republik. Ini adalah masalah senilai $3,5 triliun di Wall Street. Apakah demam AI sudah terlalu maju?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs at release before the end of this year, signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop. GRAHAM PLATNER (D),
paragraphs.cn: OpenAI dan Anthropic berencana melakukan penawaran umum perdana pada akhir tahun ini, yang menurut saya menunjukkan bahwa gelembung AI akan segera pecah. Graham Platter (Demokrat),

paragraphs.speaker: Maine Senate Candidate
paragraphs.text: To any of those who feel let down or disappointed or disillusioned, it is my job to earn your trust, faith, and support. And I will spend every day of this campaign. And if I have the privilege every day in the United States Senate doing exactly that.
paragraphs.cn: Bagi siapa pun yang merasa kecewa atau terasing, tugas saya adalah mendapatkan kepercayaan, keyakinan, dan dukungan Anda. Saya akan menghabiskan setiap hari dalam kampanye ini. Dan jika saya beruntung berada di Senat AS, saya akan melakukannya setiap hari.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Graham Platner punches his ticket. But now he's got to convince voters to choose him over a five-term senator. Good morning everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. Here is where we begin. That big statement out of Maine. Democrat Graham Platner, despite all the scandals, earned about three- fourths of primary voters' support over Governor Janet Mills. Moments after clinching the nomination, he turned his attention to his new opponent, Republican Senator Susan Collins.
paragraphs.cn: Graham Platter memenangkan nominasi. Tapi sekarang dia harus meyakinkan pemilih untuk memilihnya daripada seorang senator yang telah menjabat lima periode. Selamat pagi semuanya. Saya Audi Cornish. Mari kita mulai. Berita besar dari Maine. Graham Platter dari Partai Demokrat, meskipun dilanda skandal, memenangkan dukungan sekitar tiga perempat pemilih pendahuluan, mengalahkan Gubernur Janet Mills. Segera setelah mendapatkan nominasi, dia mengalihkan perhatiannya ke lawan barunya, Senator Partai Republik Susan Collins.

paragraphs.speaker: Platner
paragraphs.text: Susan Collins may have started -- may have started her career decades ago in Washington with good intentions, but she has become just as spineless and corrupt as the establishment she now serves. If you are an independent voice, why do you vote with Donald Trump 95 percent of the time?
paragraphs.cn: Susan Collins mungkin memulai karirnya di Washington dengan niat baik puluhan tahun lalu, tapi dia telah menjadi tidak berprinsip dan korup seperti sistem yang dia layani sekarang. Jika Anda adalah suara independen, mengapa 95% dari waktu Anda memilih Donald Trump?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Platner has a lot of work to do to get Mills voters on his side.
paragraphs.cn: Platter perlu bekerja keras untuk membuat pendukung Mills berpihak padanya.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: I couldn't honestly support Platner, even though some of the things that he says are really good.
paragraphs.cn: Saya benar-benar tidak bisa mendukung Platter, meskipun beberapa hal yang dia katakan cukup bagus.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: I don't think he's ready to be a senator. I'm leaning towards Collins unless the guy shows something else, but I just -- he's not there yet. And --
paragraphs.cn: Saya tidak berpikir dia siap menjadi senator. Saya cenderung mendukung Collins, kecuali orang itu menunjukkan hal lain, tapi saya hanya -- dia belum sampai di situ. Dan --

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: And so, what will you do in November if its Platner versus Collins?
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, jika November adalah antara Plattner dan Collins, apa yang akan Anda lakukan?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: That's a really good question. I'm a Democrat, but I voted for Susan Collins several times, because I think that she does care about the state of Maine. But it's going to be a real problem for me in November.
paragraphs.cn: Itu pertanyaan yang bagus. Saya seorang Demokrat, tetapi saya telah memilih Susan Collins beberapa kali karena saya pikir dia benar-benar peduli pada Maine. Tapi November akan menjadi masalah nyata bagi saya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, today in the group chat, Mike Leon, host of the "Can We Please Talk" podcast; Jared Stepman, columnist with "The Daily Signal"; and Max Rose, former Democratic congressman from New York. And Max, I'm actually going to start with you, because you're with VoteVets, which means you guys had a dog in the fight in this race. You guys have been giving a lot of money across the country. And what is it? Let me just show what -- what's at stake here. Democrats' chances of flipping the Maine Senate seat.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, hari ini dalam obrolan grup, ada Mike Leone, pembawa acara podcast 'Can We Talk?', Jared Steppman, kolumnis untuk 'The Daily Signal', dan mantan anggota DPR Demokrat dari New York, Max Rose. Max, saya sebenarnya ingin bertanya kepada Anda terlebih dahulu, karena Anda bekerja di VoteVets, yang berarti Anda juga memiliki kepentingan dalam pemilihan ini. Anda telah menginvestasikan banyak uang secara nasional. Jadi bagaimana keadaannya? Biarkan saya menunjukkan --  -- apa yang menjadi kuncinya di sini. Peluang Demokrat untuk membalikkan kursi Senat Maine.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It was pretty good. Maybe this has jeopardized it. We're going to show people a chart while you're talking so you can see what's at stake. Is this race as close as you all hoped it would be, now that Graham has struggled with these scandals? MAX ROSE (D),
paragraphs.cn: Awalnya cukup bagus. Mungkin ini telah terancam. Kami akan menunjukkan grafik kepada orang-orang saat Anda berbicara sehingga mereka bisa melihat apa yang dipertaruhkan. Karena Graham bergumul dengan skandal-skandal ini, apakah pemilihan ini sedekat yang Anda harapkan? Max Rose (Demokrat):

paragraphs.speaker: Former New York Congressman
paragraphs.text: The race is always going to be close. Because --
paragraphs.cn: Pemilihan ini akan selalu ketat. Karena --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Not with a five-term senator, it's not. A lot of times it can be a blowout, right? Like what -- how do you look at what he needs to accomplish?
paragraphs.cn: Untuk seorang senator yang menjabat lima periode, tidak seperti itu. Seringkali bisa menjadi satu sisi, kan? Seperti --  -- Bagaimana menurut Anda apa yang perlu dia capai?

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.text: Well, I think that we're saying the same thing --
paragraphs.cn: Yah, saya pikir kita mengatakan hal yang sama --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.text: -- in essence, right? Susan Collins is tough to beat. What I would say about the Susan Collins race is that we've seen in the past, is you're not going to win this seat by electing a Susan Collins light or putting forward a Susan Collins light in a race. You are going to beat her by energizing your base, particularly in a midterm election.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- pada dasarnya, kan? Susan Collins sulit dikalahkan. Apa yang ingin saya katakan tentang kampanye Susan Collins adalah, yang kita lihat di masa lalu adalah, Anda tidak bisa memenangkan kursi ini dengan memilih 'Susan Collins versi ringan' atau menampilkan 'Susan Collins versi ringan' dalam pemilihan. Anda mengalahkannya dengan memotivasi basis Anda, terutama dalam pemilihan paruh waktu.

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.text: There is no doubt, though, that this election has got to be a referendum on Susan Collins: on the ways in which she has voted to empower Donald Trump; on her refusal to hold Donald Trump accountable; on the ways in which she has said, I might be fair, but I want to take healthcare away from millions of people. Says the right thing often; does the wrong thing very frequently.
paragraphs.cn: Namun, tidak diragukan lagi, pemilihan ini harus menjadi referendum tentang Susan Collins: tentang cara dia memilih untuk memberdayakan Donald Trump; tentang cara dia menolak untuk meminta pertanggungjawaban Donald Trump; tentang apa yang dia katakan, saya mungkin adil, tetapi saya ingin mengambil perawatan kesehatan dari jutaan orang. Dia sering mengatakan hal yang benar; tetapi sangat sering melakukan hal yang salah.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. So, this is very much the message we hear over and over again. And I love that Platner in front of him, it said, "They don't know Maine." You know what I mean? Usually not his own name, not a bunch of other things. It was like, hello, media, this one is for you. I want to play two clips back-to-back, because it sort of, I think, represents something of this race. First, I'm going to play for you J.D. Vance and how he talks about Susan Collins.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Jadi, itulah pesan yang kami dengar berulang kali. Saya suka papan nama Plattner di depannya yang bertuliskan 'Mereka tidak tahu Maine'. Anda tahu maksud saya? Biasanya bukan namanya sendiri, atau banyak hal lainnya. Seperti, media, ini untuk Anda. Saya ingin memutar dua klip audio berturut-turut, karena saya pikir ini mewakili beberapa karakteristik pemilihan ini. Pertama, saya akan memutar klip J.D. Vance berbicara tentang Susan Collins.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And then I'm going to play AOC and how she's talking about Platner. You tell me how you hear the tone of these. J.D. VANCE (R),
paragraphs.cn: Kemudian saya akan memutar klip AOC berbicara tentang Plattner. Anda memberi tahu saya bagaimana Anda merasakan nada-nada ini. J.D. Vance (Republik):

paragraphs.speaker: U.S. Vice President
paragraphs.text: Here's the thing I'll say about Susan Collins, is sometimes I get frustrated with Susan Collins. I almost wish that she was more partisan. But the thing I love about Susan is she is independent, because Maine is an independent state. And frankly, if she was as partisan as I sometimes wish that she was, she would not be a good fit for the people of Maine. So, let's give a shout-out to Susan Collins, who's doing a great job.
paragraphs.cn: Apa yang akan saya katakan tentang Susan Collins adalah, kadang-kadang saya frustrasi dengannya. Saya hampir berharap dia lebih partisan. Tapi apa yang saya suka dari Susan adalah dia independen, karena Maine adalah negara bagian yang independen. Terus terang, jika dia partisan seperti yang kadang-kadang saya harapkan, dia tidak akan cocok untuk rakyat Maine. Jadi, mari kita bersorak untuk Susan Collins, dia melakukan pekerjaan dengan baik.

paragraphs.speaker: Ocasio-Cortez
paragraphs.text: When it comes to the substance of this reporting, obviously, there's a lot in that behavior that's really challenging. It's hard to stomach, you know, in some of it. But -- but at the end of the day, I think that this is a choice. If that choice on the ballot is between that and a senator who's voted to take healthcare away from millions of Americans, that's the situation that we have to weigh.
paragraphs.cn: Regarding the substance of this report, clearly there is a lot that is very disturbing in that behavior. Some of it is hard to accept. But ultimately, I think it's a choice. If the choice on the ballot is between that and a senator who would strip millions of Americans of their healthcare, that's the situation we have to weigh.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I played these, because one is like, it's hard to stomach. And the other is like, I get frustrated. Shout-out though, anyway, because I guess Maine. And I was, like, these are not ringing endorsements. MIKE LEON, HOST, "CAN WE PLEASE TALK?"
paragraphs.cn: I played these because one is hard to accept and the other frustrates me. But still, kudos because I think it's Maine. I thought, these are not enthusiastic endorsements. Mike Leon, host, "Can we talk about it?"

paragraphs.speaker: Podcast
paragraphs.text: But Audie, not only are they not ringing endorsements, the voter that we played in the package before listen to her tone. Maine independents are the second largest party in Maine.
paragraphs.cn: But Audi, not only is it not an enthusiastic endorsement, listen to the tone of that voter we played in the earlier clip. Independents in Maine are the second largest party in the state.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. And a woman, in particular.
paragraphs.cn: Yes, especially women.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.text: And a woman and a Democrat who said she voted for Susan Collins, against her will, it sounded like, from that thing. So -- but those are -- those are some of the things that we're going to have to consider now. It's really about the character quality is what something that Graham Platner has been trying to say: Forget about that. I'm from here. It's how she's voted, to Max's point.
paragraphs.cn: A female Democrat, she said she voted for Susan Collins, but it sounded like against her will. So -- -- but these are some of the things we have to consider now. It's really about character traits, which is what Graham Plattner has been trying to say: forget that. I'm from here. What matters is her voting record, as Max said.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.text: But then also you hear the voters, and they're, like, a little, still unsure because of those character issues with Platner. So, it's going to be really interesting.
paragraphs.cn: But you also hear voters, they're still a bit uncertain because of Plattner's character issues. So, this will be very interesting.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And he's been telling a story for a while of, like, you know, I was -- went through difficult times. Now I'm a person who has changed. I want to ask one more thing. Collins and Trump, that's not a tight bond. OK? Can I just put it that way? Here's a list I'm going to play for you. Just some moments over the past couple of months where you hear her talking around Trump policies and where she stands on them. SEN. SUSAN COLLINS (R-ME): The 60 days is a trigger.
paragraphs.cn: And he's been telling a story, like, you know, I've been through hard times. Now I'm a changed person. I want to ask one more thing. Collins and Trump, not close. Okay? Can I say that? Here's a list, I'm going to play it for you. Just some moments from the past few months, you hear her talking about Trump's policies and her stance on them. Senator Susan Collins (R-ME): 60 days is a trigger point.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: After 60 days, in my view, the president has to obtain congressional approval or Congress can block it. I do not believe that individuals who were convicted of violence against police officers on January 6th should be entitled to reimbursement for their legal fees. Concerned about the cuts in Medicaid and the impact on my state, but other states, as well. I've talked with the secretary of homeland security.
paragraphs.cn: After 60 days, in my view, the president must get congressional approval, or Congress can block it. I don't think people convicted of violently assaulting police on January 6 should be entitled to reimbursement of legal fees. I'm concerned about the impact of Medicaid cuts on my state and others. I've spoken with the Secretary of Homeland Security.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I have strongly recommended to her that there be a pause on the enforcement surge in both Maine and in Minnesota.
paragraphs.cn: I strongly advised her to suspend enforcement actions in Maine and Minnesota.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, I've chased her in the hallways before, too. She often ends up voting with Republicans, whatever that party line is. paragraphs.cn: Bagaimana dengan pemilih? Apakah Anda akan memberitahu pemilih?

paragraphs.speaker: Marx
paragraphs.text: Theres no need. I don't think that's important.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak perlu. Saya rasa itu tidak penting.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. Group chat is back. I wanted to talk about this because we started the hour talking about Maine, right, and Graham Platner and being like, voters, what are they thinking? In this Republican primary right now, the fundraising, Victor Marx is at $2.7 million.
paragraphs.cn: Baik. Grup chat kembali. Saya ingin membahas ini karena kita memulai jam ini dengan membicarakan Maine, kan, dan Graham Platter, apa yang dipikirkan pemilih? Anda tahu, apa yang terjadi? Apa dampak dari skandal-skandal ini? Dan kemudian saya melihat lebih banyak laporan tentang apa yang terjadi di Colorado. Untuk memberi Anda gambaran. Dalam pemilihan pendahuluan Partai Republik ini, dalam hal penggalangan dana, Victor Marx memiliki $2,7 juta.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The next closest person is at around $500,000, who is a state senator. I know you're just kind of clocking into this, Jarrett, but help me understand why he's so far ahead in the money. That means people are the donors. The people with pockets are giving to him. JARRETT STEPMAN, CONTRIBUTOR, "THE DAILY SIGNAL": Yes. In fairness, that may shift a bit after this moment, seeing him not as credible candidate.
paragraphs.cn: Peringkat kedua memiliki sekitar $500.000, seorang senator negara bagian. Saya tahu Anda baru bergabung dalam diskusi, Jarrett, tapi bantu saya mengerti mengapa dia unggul jauh dalam pendanaan. Ini berarti orang-orang adalah donor. Orang kaya memberinya uang. Kontributor The Daily Signal, Jarrett Stepman: Ya. Secara adil, setelah melihat bahwa dia bukan kandidat yang kredibel, hal itu mungkin berubah.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, that's a shift by about a million bucks. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, itu sekitar selisih satu juta dolar. Ya.

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: It would -- it would have to be quite a bit.
paragraphs.cn: That would require a lot of changes.

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: And, frankly, it -- Colorado, look, they haven't elected a Republican governor in that state since --
paragraphs.cn: And, frankly, Colorado, you see, they haven't elected a Republican governor since...

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: So, it's been a long time. I think you sometimes see this in states that have gone one way for a long time. You get sometimes candidates who are not particularly credible. There's a sort of a desperation saying, this guy seems like an outsider. He's, said, look, we saw this in Maine, somebody who's an outsider, in that case actually had a huge amount of success against a political establishment.
paragraphs.cn: So, it's been a long time. I think sometimes you see this in states that have leaned one way for a long time. Sometimes there are candidates who are not very credible. There's a sense of desperation that says this guy looks like an outsider. He says, look, we saw it in Maine, an outsider, actually in that case, had great success against the political establishment.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Can I tell you how far outside --

paragraphs.cn: I'm going to -- -- I need to know, what's this exorcism bit? Is it an internet joke? Or are people a little, you know, I don't know what to think. But this is what I saw. Victor Marks (Republican),

paragraphs.cn: When you say I perform exorcisms.

paragraphs.cn: Yes. You describe yourself as a, quote, reluctant exorcist.

paragraphs.cn: Yes. Describe how your exorcism works, when you command the demon to leave.

paragraphs.text: Well, that's a joke. We identify the demon, like Jesus did. We asked what their name is. And then we give them commands. And they very frequently and typically will answer through the person's mind. They'll tell us what they hear. And then we destroy them, banish them to perfect judgment from Christ.
paragraphs.cn: Well, it's a joke. We identify demons, like Jesus did. We ask their names. Then we give them commands. They often and usually answer through the person's mind. They tell us what they heard. Then we destroy them, banishing them to the perfect judgment of Christ.

paragraphs.speaker: Clark

paragraphs.cn: If you are elected governor, will you continue to perform exorcisms while in office?

paragraphs.speaker: Marx

paragraphs.cn: I will never stop praying for people.

paragraphs.cn: So, Audi, remember earlier in the first paragraph we discussed candidate qualities? Shout out to Kyle Clark, reporter at 9News Denver. I love watching you on social media. He's done a lot on this.

paragraphs.cn: He actually became a social media star because of this coverage.

And this morning about a candidate, I mean, come on. (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: He really did. Because of this and the backlash. He asked different candidates different questions, giving them factual examples. By doing so, I think he really exposed the candidates. As you said, he has $2.7 million in campaign donations. Considering this person's track record, history, and what he just said in the interview, that should shock all of us. paragraphs.cn: Yes. But, you know, I also thought, okay, Marine, if he's talking about war, maybe people are interested in that. paragraphs.cn: Yes. Yes, as a Democrat, I want to say to my Republican friends and colleagues, that's your guy. That's your candidate. They should vote for him. They should rally for him.

paragraphs.cn: They should -- -- we should (inaudible). This is their path to finally take back Colorado. (Cross talk)

paragraphs.cn: Listen. No, okay, what you're saying, I think you find it funny, but, however, this is Lauren Boebert from Colorado explaining why she thinks this is a decent path. Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-CO): So, I think now in Colorado, we need a motivator who can lead. Motivation must be key in any Republican campaign, especially in off-year elections. I believe Victor Marx will put qualified people in positions. I am ready to help him. I am not disparaging any other candidates.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I have endorsed Victor Marx, but that's not something that I've turned the volume way up on. I want candidates to be able to professionally run their own race. I do not like the hateful back and forth that I see with Republicans.
paragraphs.cn: Saya mendukung Victor Marx, tapi itu bukan sesuatu yang saya umumkan. Saya berharap kandidat bisa menjalankan kampanye secara profesional. Saya tidak suka melihat pertengkaran penuh kebencian di antara Partai Republik.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish

paragraphs.cn: Saya bilang, jangan (tidak jelas) perbesar suaranya.

paragraphs.cn: Ya, itu benar-benar bumerang. Saya punya satu hal lagi tentang orang luar. Ini menyangkut anggota Kongres yang berharap masuk ke kantor baru tahun depan. Mereka tertinggal. Tadi malam, Partai Republik Nancy Mace berada di peringkat kelima dalam pemilihan pendahuluan gubernur Partai Republik di South Carolina. Dia tidak mendapat dukungan presiden dalam kampanye. Dia pikir itu karena dia memilih untuk merilis file Epstein, yang mungkin akhirnya menyebabkan kekalahannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: NANCY MACE (R-SC): I chose to expose the abusers of children, and apparently I chose wrong if the goal was winning an election. I'm at peace with that because when a candidate is OK with corruption, when a candidate is OK with cover ups, something is broken. And that's not political opinion, that is a moral emergency.
paragraphs.cn: Nancy Mace (R-SC): Saya memilih untuk mengungkap pelaku kekerasan anak, jelas jika tujuannya adalah memenangkan pemilu, saya salah pilih. Saya merasa tenang dengan itu, karena ketika seorang kandidat mentolerir korupsi, ketika seorang kandidat mentolerir penutupan, ada sesuatu yang rusak. Itu bukan pandangan politik, itu krisis moral.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish

Is that reflected here, or is this really candidate quality?

Apakah ini tercermin di sini, atau ini benar-benar masalah kualitas kandidat?

paragraphs.cn: I want to go back to our discussion in Maine with Susan Collins, talking about issues, and Plattner, talking to Maine people about issues. I don't think South Carolinians hear Nancy Mace talking about those. It's more the headlines, and her getting the microphone -- --

paragraphs.cn: Because the primary base voters are very extreme, and they are now severely out of touch with independents and moderates nationwide. But Nancy Mace goes further. paragraphs.text: Yes. That's a good point, you're not effective if you're at odds with everyone.

paragraphs.cn: You won't be effective, and you'll be contagious (sound). She will continue this. Obviously, if she's done it this far, I don't think she'll stop. But I -- -- she has completely fallen out with President Trump, that's clear. So, what do you have to lose now?
